message: docs: add gzip vs TurboQuant comparison — gzip saves 7%, TQ saves 84%

files.patch:
@@ -11,10 +11,10 @@

turboquant-wasm

Shrink embedding vectors 6x. Search them without decompressing.

- Vector compression has existed for years (FAISS, ScaNN, PQ), but they require - a training step on your dataset first. TurboQuant (Google Research, ICLR 2026) skips that — - just encode any vector immediately, no codebook, no training. Three lines of code: - encode(), decode(), dot(). + Float32 embeddings don't compress well — gzip only shrinks them 7% (7.3 MB → 6.8 MB). + TurboQuant (Google Research, ICLR 2026) reduces them 6x (7.3 MB → 1.2 MB) and lets you + search directly on compressed data without decompressing. No training step, no codebook — + just encode(), decode(), dot(). This npm package runs it in the browser via WASM with relaxed SIMD.
